apt_groups.name: Lazarus Group
apt_groups.notable_incident: 2025 Bybit $1.5B cryptocurrency heist via multi-signature wallet compromise
apt_groups.description: Lazarus Group is a North Korean state-sponsored APT group active since 2009. In 2025, signature malware includes PondRAT (fileless modular Trojan), ThemeForestRAT (LOLBin hollowing), and RemotePE (in-memory lateral movement). They employ reflective DLL injection, PowerShell stagers, and encrypted C2 channels.

apt_groups.name: APT29 / Cozy Bear
apt_groups.notable_incident: SolarWinds supply chain attack (2020) impacting 18,000+ organizations, 6-12 month dwell time
apt_groups.description: Russian SVR-linked group known for unprecedented supply chain attacks. Defining characteristic: strategic patience with long dwell times. Extensively uses DNS tunneling and encrypted C2 channels.

apt_groups.name: APT41 / Wicked Panda
apt_groups.notable_incident: 2025 exploitation targeting U.S. policy non-profit, using Google Calendar API as covert C2
apt_groups.description: Chinese dual-use threat group conducting both espionage and financial attacks. In 2025, signature tools include Deed RAT, TOUGHPROGRESS (Google Calendar C2), and PatoRAT. Aggressively exploits public-facing application vulnerabilities.

apt_groups.name: AI-Enhanced Threat Actor (Emerging)
apt_groups.notable_incident: MITRE 2025: AI-abuse activities moved from speculation to recorded, attributed events
apt_groups.description: Emerging threat: attackers leveraging LLM Agents (Claude Code, OpenCode, CyberStrike-AI) for automated reconnaissance, code generation, and multi-path lateral exploration. Characterized by LLM-generated code with explanatory comments and trial-and-error path switching.

sources: HiveForce Labs Weekly Threat Digest Nov 2025
